blocks:
Surah Al-Fil

Five Verses by Consensus بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(1) أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصْحابِ الْفِيلِ. (1) Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt with the People of the Elephant? (2) أَلَمْ يَجْعَلْ كَيْدَهُمْ فِي تَضْلِيلٍ (2) Did He not make their stratagem go astray, (in destroying the Ka'bah) (in loss and nullification) by destroying them and magnifying its status. (3) وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْراً أَبابِيلَ (3) and send against them flocks of birds (groups/flocks). (4) تَرْمِيهِمْ بِحِجارَةٍ مِنْ سِجِّيلٍ (4) pelting them with stones of Sijjil, (from hardened clay, the Arabicized form of سنگ گل). (5) فَجَعَلَهُمْ كَعَصْفٍ مَأْكُولٍ (5) making them like straw eaten up. (like the leaves of a crop afflicted by blight (الاكّال), or whose grain was eaten leaving it empty, or like straw (تبن) eaten by livestock (الدوابّ)). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was revealed concerning the Abyssinians (الحبشة) when they came with the elephant to destroy the Ka'bah. When they brought it near the gate of the Mosque, ‘Abd al-Muttalib said to it: "Do you know where they are leading you?" It replied with its head: "No." He said: "They brought you to destroy the Ka'bah of Allah. Will you do that?" It replied with its head: "No." The Abyssinians exerted themselves to make it enter the Mosque, but it refused. So they attacked it with swords and cut it up. And Allah ** وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْراً أَبابِيلَ (sent against them flocks of birds). He said: Some followed others, ** تَرْمِيهِمْ بِحِجارَةٍ مِنْ سِجِّيلٍ (pelting them with stones of Sijjil). He said: With every bird were three stones: one stone in its beak and two stones in its claws (مخالسه). They fluttered over their heads and threw the stones into their brains, so the stone entered their brains and exited their rear ends (أدبارهم), and their bodies disintegrated. Thus, they became as He said: ** فَجَعَلَهُمْ كَعَصْفٍ مَأْكُولٍ (making them like straw eaten up). He said: Al-ʿAsf (العصف) is straw (التّبن), and al-Ma’kul (المأكول) is what remains of its residue (فضله).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), is a similar meaning in two narrations, with additions and variations in their wording. In one of them, He (peace be upon him) said: And Allah sent against them birds like swallows (الخطاطيف), having in their beaks a stone like a lentil or similar. It would hover over a man's head, then release the stone upon his head, and it would exit his rear end (دبره), until none of them remained except one man who fled. He began telling people what he had seen when one of those birds appeared above him. He raised his head and said: "This is one of those birds." The bird came until it hovered over his head, then dropped the stone upon him, and it exited his rear end (دبره), and he died.

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: ** وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْراً (and send against them flocks of birds). He said: It was a low-flying bird (طير سافّ) that came to them from the direction of the sea. Their heads were like the heads of beasts of prey (السّباع), and their claws were like the claws of beasts of prey among the birds. With every bird were three stones: two stones in its feet and one stone in its beak. They began pelting them with these stones until their bodies became afflicted with smallpox (جدرت أجسادهم), and He killed them with it. Nothing of smallpox (الجدري) had ever occurred before that, nor had they seen such birds before that day or after it. He said: And whoever escaped from them that day set off until they reached Hadramawt (حضرموت), which is a valley short of Yemen. Allah sent a flood (سيلاً) upon them and drowned them all. He said: No water had ever been seen in that valley for fifteen years before that day. He said: And for that reason, it was named Hadramawt (حضرموت) (Death is Present) when they died therein.

In al-Ilal, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), is that which is close to it.

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, Abrahah ibn Yaksūm led the elephant to the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام) to destroy it before the advent of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny). ‘Abd al-Muttalib said: "Indeed, this House has a Lord Who protects it." Then he gathered the people of Mecca and supplicated. This was after Sayf ibn Dhī Yazan had informed him. So Allah ** أَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْراً أَبابِيلَ (sent against them flocks of birds) and repelled them from Mecca and its people. In al-Amali, there are additions to this story. It was said that the reason for it was that Abrahah ibn al-Sabbāh al-Ashram, the king of Yemen on behalf of As-hamah al-Najashi, built a church in San‘a’ and named it al-Qullays (القليس). He intended to divert the pilgrims (الحاجّ) to it. Then a man from Kinānah went out and relieved himself (فقعد فيها) in it at night. This angered him, so he swore that he would destroy the Ka'bah. Thus, he set out with his army, accompanied by a strong elephant named Mahmūd, and so on until the end of the story.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites ** أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ (Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt) in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه), every plain (سهل), mountain (جبل), and clod of earth (مدر) will testify for him on the Day of Resurrection that he was one of the performers of prayer (المصلّين). And a caller will announce on the Day of Resurrection: "You have spoken truly concerning My servant. I have accepted your testimony for him and against him. Admit him into Paradise and do not hold him accountable, for he is one of those Whom Allah loved and Whose deed He loved." It has preceded that this Surah and the one following it are recited together in the prayer.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Ayyashi, from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both), He said: ** أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ (Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt) and ** لِإِيلَافِ قُرَيْشٍ (For the familiarity of Quraysh) are a single Surah. He said: And it is narrated that Ubayy ibn Ka‘b did not separate them in his Mushaf (مصحفه).
